confirmedFindings.new_string: ### 1.5.1 ESTATE — the Kurosawa *gōshi* household (MAIN — a fresh rank ladder PER TIER)

confirmedFindings.title: "ronin" vs "*rōnin*" used inconsistently; the macron-lint cannot catch the drift
confirmedFindings.evidence: Allow-list (4127-4128): "naturalized English exonyms — *shogun, ronin, yokai, samurai, Osaka, daimyō*… are exempt and may stay in their common English form." Yet cast/bestiary prose uses the macron form: (545) "A scarred *rōnin* enforcer", (3616) "Masterless *rōnin* / road toughs", while mechanical/table prose uses plain: (1172) "human bandits/ronin (T2)", (4095) "boars/wolves/monkeys/bandits/ronin/smugglers". Because "ronin" is allow-listed, BOTH "ronin" and "rōnin" pass the §6.6 lint, so the inconsistency will silently propagate into shipped text.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: The same enemy/character class is spelled two ways depending on whether it sits in flavour prose or a stat table; readers and the eventual content registries will inherit both. The lint that guarantees romanization consistency is explicitly blind to this term, so only an authoring decision can settle it.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Pick one canonical form and normalize all ~13 occurrences (and the bestiary registry), then state the rule beside the allow-list.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed: docs/prd.md uses both plain "ronin" (lines 1172, 1916, 2022, 3012, 4095, 4375, 4629, 4655) and italic-macron "*rōnin*" (lines 545, 1180, 1205, 3509, 3616) for the same enemy/character class. The allow-list at lines 4127-4129 exempts "ronin" from the macron lint, so §6.6 genuinely cannot flag the drift — the lint-blindness claim holds. The contradiction is soft (the allow-list says exonyms "may" stay plain, so it permits rather than mandates), but the same term spelled two ways across flavour prose vs stat tables is a real internal inconsistency that will propagate into content registries. Downgraded from medium to low: impact is purely cosmetic/romanization with no mechanical, balance, or scope effect (~13 textual occurrences). Not trivial-apply because picking the canonical form is a taste/authoring judgement — the italic *rōnin* sits among in-world romaji (*kodachi*, *sekisho*) in the same sentences, so blindly normalizing to plain could strip intended flavour.
confirmedFindings.question: "ronin" appears 8x as plain ASCII (mechanical/table prose) and 5x as italic *rōnin* (cast/bestiary flavour prose); the macron lint is blind to it because "ronin" is on the §6.6 allow-list. Should "ronin" be (a) a naturalized English exonym — normalize all occurrences to plain "ronin" (consistent with its allow-list siblings yokai/samurai), or (b) an in-world romaji term — normalize to italic *rōnin* (consistent with *kodachi*/*sekisho* in the same flavour lines, but then it must come off the macron-lint allow-list so the lint can enforce it)? Pick one, normalize all ~13 occurrences plus the enemies.ts bestiary entry, and state the rule beside the allow-list.

confirmedFindings.title: No glossary anywhere, and several Edo-domain terms are never glossed for a non-Japanese player
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md — no glossary section exists (grep "glossary/codex/lore-book" = 0 hits in prd.md); unglossed terms: goyōkin (377), sankin-kōtai (423, 470), inter-han (422), satoyama (220, 237); imprecise gloss: meibutsu (37, 377)
confirmedFindings.evidence: §1.6.1 (377): "TREASURY (debt→solvency→creditworthiness, *goyōkin*)" — *goyōkin* (a forced domain levy/loan) is never defined. §1.5 (423): "the *rusui-yaku* under the daimyō's *sankin-kōtai*" — *sankin-kōtai* (alternate-attendance) is used as if known. §1.5 (422): "inter-*han* markets" — *han* (domain/fief) never glossed. §1.5 (220): "the near-satoyama" never glossed. *meibutsu* is only ever equated to its value ("*meibutsu* = silk / sericulture", 37/377), never defined as "a region's signature product." The accessibility plan (§2.21 / §6.11) covers UI a11y (text scale, colourblind, keyboard) only — nothing about explaining the game's Japanese vocabulary.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: This is a free, general-audience itch.io game steeped in Edo vocabulary. Most load-bearing terms ARE glossed inline (koku/rice, mon/coin, hiyatoi=day-labourer, monban=gate-guard), but the upper-tier economic/political flavour (goyōkin, sankin-kōtai, han) is opaque to a non-Japanese-speaking player, and there is no in-game codex/glossary nor a verifier rule guaranteeing gloss-on-first-use.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Decide whether the game needs an in-game glossary/codex (terms explained when first surfaced) and/or a §6.6 "gloss-on-first-use" verifier check, and ensure goyōkin / sankin-kōtai / han / satoyama / meibutsu each get a one-line in-world gloss.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Factual core verified: prd.md has no glossary/codex (grep = 0), the accessibility canon (§2.21, §6.11) covers UI a11y only (text scale, colourblind, keyboard/touch, pause, motion, ARIA, mute) with nothing on explaining Japanese vocabulary, and the §6.6 verifier has a macron lint but no gloss-on-first-use rule. goyōkin, sankin-kōtai, and inter-han are genuinely never defined inline. So the gap — no committed glossary mechanism and no gloss-on-first-use guarantee — is real and not covered elsewhere. Downgraded from medium to low for two reasons the auditor under-weighted: (1) the three truly-opaque terms all live in deferred T3/T4 stub/roadmap content, not v1 player-facing live mechanics (v1 ships T0–T2 plus a T3 cliff-hanger stub), so no v1 player meets them as a working system; and (2) satoyama is loosely contextualized in-world ("the near hills open — foraging groves," 1862; "shared wilderness," 451) and meibutsu is flagged "signature" beside every use (377, 382), so the "never glossed / imprecise" claims there are weak. The finding itself concedes the load-bearing v1 terms (koku, mon, genin, monban, hiyatoi) are glossed. It is a minor open-design question, not a contradiction; cannot be a clerical fix (deciding glossary vs. mandatory inline gloss vs. accept-opacity is a design call), so needs-human.
confirmedFindings.question: For non-Japanese players, how should Edo-domain vocabulary be made legible — and is anything needed for v1? Note the opaque terms (goyōkin, sankin-kōtai, inter-han) are all T3/T4 roadmap/stub content, while v1's live terms (koku, mon, genin, monban, satoyama, meibutsu) are already glossed inline or in-world. Options: (a) do nothing for v1 and add a one-line in-world gloss to each upper-tier term when T3/T4 is actually built; (b) commit now to a §6.6 "gloss-on-first-use" verifier rule that flags any flavour term surfaced to the player without an adjacent gloss; (c) commit to an in-game codex/glossary panel (terms explained when first surfaced); (d) accept that some upper-tier flavour terms stay opaque by design. Currently the PRD commits to none of these.

confirmedFindings.title: "Standing" is overloaded: the pillar "Standing & Office" (官威) vs the rank-gate "Combat Standing" vs "Arms standing"
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md — pillar name: 378, 1436; "Combat Standing" rank-gate: 223, 1803, 1908, 1933, 3012; "Arms standing": 1804, 1908
confirmedFindings.evidence: Pillar (378): "**Standing & Office** (political/territorial) | 官威 *kan'i*". Rank-gate of a DIFFERENT pillar (1803): "| **R5 — Gate-guard (*monban*)** | `RANK` Combat Standing (stand a real watch…)" — "Combat Standing" is the rung-gate type feeding the Arms (武威) pillar, e.g. (1908) "combat-earned Arms standing at valley scale". So one pillar is literally named "**Standing** & Office" (官威) while the *other* combat pillar's promotion requirement is "Combat **Standing**" and its deeds are "Arms **standing**" — three distinct mechanics sharing the word, plus the generic "recognized standing"/"estate standing".
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: A player or reader can't tell from the word "standing" whether "Combat Standing" feeds the Arms pillar or the pillar actually named "Standing & Office." The collision is precisely the "one word, two meanings" trap, on the game's hero scoreboard (the four pillars) and its rank gates.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Rename one axis to disambiguate — e.g. the rung-gate "Combat Standing" → "Combat Rank" / "Martial Prowess," or rename the 官威 pillar's English label so "Standing" denotes exactly one concept.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Verified against docs/prd.md. Line 378 names a pillar "Standing & Office" (官威 kan'i, political/territorial). Lines 199-201 and 1908/1911 define "Combat Standing" as the rank-gating meter that feeds a DIFFERENT pillar — Arms (武威), line 376 — and line 1908 calls its deeds "Arms standing" ("combat-earned Arms standing at valley scale"). So the word "standing" simultaneously denotes (1) the 官威 pillar's English label, (2) the Arms-pillar rung-gate "Combat Standing", and (3) Arms deeds "Arms standing", on top of generic uses ("house's standing", "broker standing", "estate standing"). A reader genuinely cannot infer from "Combat Standing" whether it feeds Arms or the pillar literally named "Standing & Office." The collision sits on the game's hero scoreboard (four pillars) and its rank gates. Real ambiguity. Fix is a naming/design choice (which axis keeps "standing", what the others become) with downstream effects across the PRD and UI labels, so needs-human, not trivial-apply. Medium severity is appropriate — it's a clarity/UX risk, not a mechanical contradiction.
confirmedFindings.question: The word "Standing" currently labels three distinct mechanics: the 官威 pillar ("Standing & Office", a hero-scoreboard bar), the Arms-pillar rank-gate meter ("Combat Standing", lines 200/223/1908), and Arms deeds ("Arms standing", line 1908) — plus generic uses ("house's standing", "broker standing"). On the four-pillar scoreboard a reader can't tell whether "Combat Standing" feeds Arms or the pillar named "Standing". Which concept keeps the word "Standing", and what do the others get renamed to? Options: (a) rename the Arms rank-gate "Combat Standing" → "Combat Rank" / "Martial Prowess" and "Arms standing" → "Arms deeds", leaving "Standing & Office" sole owner; or (b) rename the 官威 pillar's English label (e.g. "Office & Authority") so "standing" denotes only the earned martial/rank meters.

confirmedFindings.title: Persisted RNG shape `{ seed, counter }` cannot represent the canon's named sub-streams — reload and repeated encounters are non-reproducible
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §6.7 L4146 + L4151-4152; §6.4 L4043; §6.8 L4170; vs canon docs/history/decisions.md L138/L140 (D-013a) and prd_human_feedback.md H4 L268-269
confirmedFindings.evidence: §6.7 L4146 pins the persisted shape to a SINGLE scalar counter: "(`state.rng = { seed, counter }`) and is **saved & loaded**". §6.7 L4151-4152 then derives sub-streams by drawing from that one counter: "For independent sub-streams (e.g. a fight's rolls vs. weather) we **derive child RNGs by splitting** from the parent counter, so each subsystem is reproducible without coupling". But the LOCKED canon (D-013a, decisions.md L138) mandates "drawn through **named sub-streams** (combat / loot / seasonal / world-gen) so systems never share or perturb each other's draws" and its consequence (L140) is to "persist RNG seed + stream **cursors**" (plural). Two breakages: (1) a single scalar `counter` cannot store 4+ independent sub-stream cursors, so a save/reload cannot resume each named stream at its exact position; (2) deriving a child stream "from the parent counter" is order-dependent — it both contradicts the 'never perturb each other's draws' guarantee AND means two combat encounters entered at the same parent-counter value would roll identical hits/crits/loot unless every named stream keeps its own persisted, monotonic cursor. §6.4 L4062 even says the live fight stores "consumed RNG ... cannot be recomputed", but with no per-stream cursor in `state.rng` there is no defined home for the combat stream's position between fights.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: This is the core of the determinism+save lens. As written, the stored RNG surface is insufficient to reconstruct sub-stream positions on reload, and the 'split from parent counter' derivation actively violates the locked named-sub-stream independence guarantee — repeated fights/loot rolls would replay identically and a reload would diverge. It directly contradicts D-013a/H4.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Define `RngState` as `{ seed: number; cursors: Record<StreamName, number> }` (StreamName ∈ combat/loot/seasonal/world-gen/…), derive each named stream as `splitmix64(hash(seed, name))` advanced by its own persisted cursor (NOT by the parent counter), and update §6.7 L4146/4151-4152, §6.4 L4043, §6.8 L4170, §2.19 L1603 to all say 'seed + per-named-sub-stream cursors'. State explicitly that each named stream's cursor is monotonic and persisted so successive encounters are independent and reload-exact.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed in source. PRD §6.7 L4145 pins `state.rng = { seed, counter }` (single scalar) and L4151-4152 derives sub-streams "by splitting from the parent counter ... so each subsystem is reproducible without coupling"; §6.8 L4170 says "RNG (seed + counter)". Locked canon D-013a (decisions.md L138) mandates "named sub-streams (combat/loot/seasonal/world-gen) so systems never share or perturb each other's draws" with consequence (L140) "persist RNG seed + stream cursors" (plural), and H4 (prd_human_feedback.md L268) locks "splitmix64 + named sub-streams". The PRD's single-shared-parent-counter + split-from-parent model couples the named streams (order/count of one stream's splits perturbs the others via the shared counter) — internally self-contradictory ("without coupling" while sharing one counter) and a direct contradiction of the locked canon. This is the save-determinism lens's core concern. I downgrade severity from critical to high: it is a pre-implementation spec inconsistency (M0 not built yet) that is fully correctable, and a couple of the finding's secondary claims are overstated (a correct split advances the parent, so "repeated fights roll identically" is not strictly guaranteed; §6.4 L4043's comment already hedges "counter/stream"). Fix is needs-human, not trivial-apply: although the canon already fixes the DIRECTION (named sub-streams with per-stream cursors), expressing the persisted `RngState` shape and the per-name derivation scheme across §6.4/§6.7/§6.8/§2.19 carries implementation latitude (e.g. Record<StreamName,cursor> vs storing each stream's full splitmix64 state) and is not a clerical edit.
confirmedFindings.question: Canon D-013a/H4 locks "splitmix64 + named sub-streams (combat/loot/seasonal/world-gen) that never perturb each other" and "persist seed + stream cursors" (plural), but PRD §6.7 currently describes a single `{ seed, counter }` whose children are "split from the parent counter" (which couples the streams and contradicts the independence guarantee). To conform the PRD, confirm the intended persisted RNG model: (A) each named stream is seeded deterministically from the master seed (e.g. `splitmix64(hash(masterSeed, streamName))`) and carries its OWN persisted, monotonic cursor in `GameState.rng` (replacing the single counter), so streams are provably independent and reload-exact; or (B) some other scheme. If (A), should the persisted shape be `{ seed: number; cursors: Record<StreamName, number> }`, and should mid-fight combat RNG continue to live in `combat.CombatEncounterState` (§6.4 L4062) while the inter-encounter combat-stream cursor lives in `rng.cursors.combat`? Then update §6.4 L4043, §6.7 L4145/4151-4152, §6.8 L4170, and §2.19 L1603 to say "seed + per-named-sub-stream cursors" and drop the "split from the parent counter" language.

confirmedFindings.title: §6.8 (and §2.19) persisted-surface list omits stored fields §6.4 mandates: tier, the live combat encounter, the event log, and current vitals
confirmedFindings.evidence: §6.8 L4169-4173 declares "The save is the **stored** surface of `GameState`:" then enumerates "schemaVersion, RNG (seed + counter), clock, resources, producer counts, skill xp, base attributes, inventory, equipment, influence pillars (value + high-water + dents), ranks, reputation, allegiance, flags, unlocked surfaces, quest status, counts, active-effect remainders, settings." This omits four NON-derivable fields the §6.4 interface stores: (a) `tier: TierId` (L4053) — and §6.13 resolution #4 L4309-4312 explicitly locks tier as STORED, 'rather than re-deriving tier purely from pillars on every load'; (b) `combat?: CombatEncounterState` (L4062) — 'stored so a save resumes the exact encounter'; (c) `log: LogEntry[]` (L4063); (d) current vitals `character.hp` / `satiety` / `attributePoints` (L4048) — §6.8 only lists 'base attributes' (the `attributes` map), not current HP/satiety. §2.19's list (L1603-1608) likewise omits tier and the live combat encounter.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: A reader building the serializer from §6.8's authoritative-sounding enumeration would drop the live fight (breaking the mid-combat save promise of §6.4 L4062), reset the player to full HP/satiety on reload, lose the story log, and either lose tier or re-derive it (which resolution #4 explicitly rejected). This is the exact 'mid-combat save' / round-trip-completeness edge case.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Make §6.8's enumeration a faithful mirror of §6.4's stored interface by adding stored tier, the live combat encounter (when a fight is in progress), the capped event log, and current vitals (hp/satiety/attribute-points). Same fix for §2.19's list.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Verified against docs/prd.md. §6.4's authoritative stored GameState interface (L4041-4065) includes four non-derivable fields that §6.8's prose enumeration (L4169-4173) omits: (a) current vitals character.hp/satiety/attributePoints (L4048) — §6.8 only lists "base attributes" (the attributes map); (b) tier (L4053), which §6.13 resolution #4 L4309-4312 explicitly locks as STORED rather than re-derived; (c) combat?: CombatEncounterState (L4062), expressly "stored so a save resumes the exact encounter"; (d) log: LogEntry[] (L4063). The §6.8 list opens with "The save is the **stored** surface of `GameState`:" and reads as an authoritative enumeration, so its incompleteness contradicts the §6.4 interface and the explicit mid-combat-resume promise. Downgraded from high to medium because §6.8 also says "Persist only non-derivable state (§6.4)", deferring to the typed interface as source of truth, which limits real implementation risk. Fix is clerical (make the prose mirror the already-decided interface), so trivial-apply.</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="verified_old">base attributes, inventory, equipment,
  influence pillars (value + high-water + dents), ranks, reputation, allegiance, flags, unlocked surfaces,
  quest status, counts, active-effect remainders, settings.
confirmedFindings.old_string: ranks, reputation, allegiance, flags, unlocked surfaces, quest status, counts, active-effect remainders, settings.
confirmedFindings.new_string:
base attributes, current vitals (hp / satiety / attribute-points), inventory, equipment,
  influence pillars (value + high-water + dents), stored tier, ranks, reputation, allegiance, flags, unlocked surfaces,
  quest status, counts, the live combat encounter (present only while a fight is in progress), the capped event log,
  active-effect remainders, settings.

confirmedFindings.title: §6.4 GameState has no field for the player's current location/area, which §2.19 lists as persisted non-derivable state
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §6.4 L4041-4065 (no location field) vs §2.19 L1606
confirmedFindings.evidence: §2.19's persist list (L1603-1608) includes "current location" as non-derivable state to save. The §6.4 GameState interface (L4041-4065) has NO `location`/`currentArea` field — it stores `clock`, `resources`, ... `unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>` (panels/areas earned), `quests`, `counts`, etc., but nothing recording WHERE the player currently is. The `travel` intent (§6.3 L3990 `{ type: 'travel'; areaId: AreaId }`) mutates location, so it is genuinely non-derivable; `unlocked` only records which areas are reachable, not the current one. A grep of §6.4 (L4031-4078) finds no `location`/`area:`/`currentArea`.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: On reload the game cannot restore where the player is standing — a basic save round-trip failure, and an internal contradiction with §2.19 which explicitly persists it.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Add a stored field (e.g. `location: AreaId;`) to the §6.4 GameState interface and to the §6.8 persist enumeration, matching §2.19's 'current location'.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Verified against docs/prd.md. §2.19(c) L1605 lists "current location" in the persist-ONLY-non-derivable-state set. The §6.4 GameState interface (L4041-4065) enumerates the stored surface and has NO location/currentArea/areaId field (grep of the whole file confirms no such field; the only `unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>` records areas EARNED, not the current one). The §6.3 `travel` intent (L3988 `{ type: 'travel'; areaId: AreaId }`) mutates current position, so it is genuinely non-derivable and cannot be recomputed on load. This is a real internal contradiction: §2.19 persists current location but §6.4's canonical stored interface omits it, so a save round-trip cannot restore where the player stands. Downgraded high→medium: it is a single missing field in a design spec with the intent already documented (§2.19) and the type already established (§6.3 AreaId), no code exists yet, and blast radius is one line. Classified trivial-apply rather than needs-human: travel is discrete node-to-node (no continuous/mid-travel position), so `location: AreaId` is the unambiguous reconciliation with no real design trade-off.</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="verified_old">  unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>;                       // panels/screens/areas the player has earned
confirmedFindings.new_string:
unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>;                       // panels/screens/areas the player has earned
  location: AreaId;                               // the area the player currently stands in — set by the `travel` intent (§6.3); non-derivable, persisted per §2.19
confirmedFindings.question: How should current position be modelled in GameState — a single `location: AreaId`, or a richer travel/position state (e.g. mid-travel between areas)? It is missing entirely from §6.4 despite §2.19 persisting it; pick the representation and add it to the stored surface.

confirmedFindings.title: §6.4 GameState has no home for MarketState (saturation) or estate stage/structures/roster — both treated as live, persisted, non-derivable state elsewhere
confirmedFindings.evidence: §2.19 L1607-1608 persists "estate stage + built structures + roster, market saturation state". §2.4 L974 defines `MarketState { perGoodPriceIndex, saturationByGood, recoveryRate }` and §6.3 L4016 runs "the market-saturation damper recovering" per-day — i.e. an evolving, non-derivable value. §2.17 L1536 defines `EstateStage { id, econFabric[], martialFabric[], rosterCards[], pillarFloor, rankFloor … }`. The §6.4 GameState interface (L4041-4065) contains NO field for either MarketState or EstateStage/roster (grep of L4031-4078 finds no 'market'/'saturation'/'estate'/'roster').
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: The trade ≤⅓ economy and its saturation damper, plus the estate's built structures/roster, are stateful and time-evolving; if they have no slot in GameState they cannot be saved (contradicting §2.19) and would reset on reload — and the §6.4 'stored vs computed' contract is supposed to be the single authoritative shape.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Either add `market: MarketState` and `estate: EstateStage` (+ roster) fields to the §6.4 GameState interface and the §6.8 persist list, or, if any of these are intended to be derived, state so explicitly and remove them from §2.19's persist list. As written the docs contradict each other.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed by reading the cited lines. §6.4's GameState interface (L4041-4065) self-describes as "the persisted surface" / single authoritative save shape, yet contains NO field for: (a) MarketState — §2.4 L974 defines `MarketState { perGoodPriceIndex, saturationByGood, recoveryRate }` and §6.3 L4016 runs "the market-saturation damper recovering" per-day, i.e. a time-evolving value that depends on sell history and is therefore NON-derivable; or (b) EstateStage/built-structures/roster — §2.17 L1536 defines `EstateStage{...rosterCards[]...}` and `RosterMember`. §2.19 L1607-1608 explicitly lists both as persisted ("estate stage + built structures + roster, market saturation state"). Market saturation cannot be folded into existing §6.4 fields (it is a per-good price index, semantically distinct from `resources` owned-amount, `flags`, or `counts`), so at minimum it has no slot — the contradiction with §2.19 is unambiguous. Built structures/roster could arguably be encoded in `flags`/a Set, but the interface neither says so nor provides the Set, so the authoritative shape is incomplete either way. Not trivial-apply: deciding stored-vs-derived for estate stage/roster and the representation of MarketState is a design judgement.
confirmedFindings.question: §6.4's GameState interface is meant to be the single authoritative "store only non-derivable state" save shape, but it omits two things §2.19 says must persist: (1) market-saturation state (`MarketState{perGoodPriceIndex, saturationByGood, recoveryRate}` from §2.4 — a per-day-evolving, history-dependent, non-derivable value per §6.3), and (2) estate stage + built structures + roster (`EstateStage`/`RosterMember` from §2.17). For each, decide and reconcile the docs: (a) MarketState — add a dedicated `market: MarketState` field to §6.4 and §6.8's save list (recommended, since it is clearly non-derivable and has no existing home), OR explicitly state it is derived and remove it from §2.19. (b) Estate stage/built-structures/roster — are these STORED (add `estate: EstateStage` + a roster set to §6.4/§6.8), or DERIVED from `flags`/`counts` (in which case §6.4 should say so and §2.19 should stop listing them as persisted)? As written §6.4 contradicts §2.4/§2.17/§2.19.

confirmedFindings.title: Equipment references `ItemInstanceId` but no instance store exists for durability/quality; inventory is counts-only — durability has no persisted home
confirmedFindings.evidence: §6.4 L4051 equips by instance id: `equipment: Partial<Record<EquipSlot, ItemInstanceId>>`, and L4045 stores inventory as counts only: `inventory: Record<ItemId, number>; // counts (quality folded into the item key)`. There is NO `instances: Record<ItemInstanceId, …>` map anywhere in GameState (grep finds `ItemInstance` only at L4051). Yet §2.10 L1192-1196 defines per-instance, continuously-varying durability (`EquipDef { … durabilityMax … }`, `EquipState { slot → { equipDefId, durability, qualityTier } }`, `Durability { current, max, repairCost }`) and §2.19 L1606 persists "equip state + durability".
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: A per-instance value like durability cannot live in a count keyed by ItemId, and the `ItemInstanceId` referenced by `equipment` points at nothing in the stored model. So §2.19's 'durability' has no place to be saved, and equipped-item durability would be lost or undefined on reload.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Add a stored instance table (e.g. `instances: Record<ItemInstanceId, { itemId; durability; qualityTier }>`) to §6.4, or fold durability into a per-slot EquipState matching §2.10's `EquipState`, and reconcile with the counts-only inventory model.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed in docs/prd.md. §6.4 L4051 stores equipment as Partial<Record<EquipSlot, ItemInstanceId>>; grep shows ItemInstance occurs ONLY here — no instances map, no ItemInstanceId type, and inventory (L4050) is counts-only Record<ItemId, number>, so per-instance durability has no persisted home. Meanwhile §2.10 L1194 defines the canonical EquipState { slot → { equipDefId, durability, qualityTier } }, §2.19 L1605 persists 'equip state + durability', and M2b L4552-4553 explicitly implement against 'EquipState' (gearAtk fold + durability wear). The ItemInstanceId reference is a genuine dangling ref and the only place diverging from the EquipState model defined three times elsewhere. Trivial-apply because the intended model is already canonically specified (§2.10 EquipState) and §6.4 simply needs to mirror it — no design trade-off remains.
confirmedFindings.old_string: equipment: Partial<Record<EquipSlot, ItemInstanceId>>;
confirmedFindings.new_string: equipment: Partial<Record<EquipSlot, { equipDefId: EquipDefId; durability: number; qualityTier: QualityTier }>>;  // per-slot EquipState (§2.10) — durability/quality persist HERE, not in the counts-only inventory
confirmedFindings.question: How are durability-bearing equipment instances modelled in GameState — a separate `instances` map keyed by ItemInstanceId, or an EquipState carrying durability per slot? §6.4 references ItemInstanceId with no backing store while inventory is counts-only; pick the model so durability (§2.10/§2.19) can persist.

confirmedFindings.title: dtTicks integer-quantization / app-layer accumulator is unspecified — live play is frame-rate-dependent and the 'byte-identical replay' guarantee silently depends on a recorded tickScript
confirmedFindings.evidence: §6.3 L4004-4005 types `tick(state, dtTicks: number)` with the comment "dtTicks is ABSTRACT (active-play ticks)", and L4021 says "`dtTicks` is computed by the **app-layer** loop from real elapsed time *while the tab is active*" (echoed §6.9 L4216 "computes `dtTicks` from elapsed active time"). Nothing states dtTicks is an integer tick count, nor that an app-layer accumulator carries the fractional remainder so that two machines at different frame rates over the same active wall-time converge on the same integer tick total. The determinism guarantee L4025-4027 is `replay(initialState, [intents…], [tickScript…])` — i.e. it requires a recorded tickScript, implicitly conceding that live dtTicks is non-reproducible.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: Per-tick combat sub-steps draw RNG; if dtTicks is a raw fractional real-time delta, a slow/laggy machine batches larger deltas and (without a fixed-step accumulator quantizing to whole abstract ticks) consumes a different number of sub-steps → divergent fights and pacing across frame rates. The byte-identical-replay claim holds only with a recorded/quantized tickScript, which the prose does not make explicit.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: State that the app-layer loop accumulates real elapsed active time and dispatches only whole-integer abstract ticks (carrying the sub-tick remainder in app state, never in GameState), so GameState is always on an integer-tick boundary and frame-rate-independent; clarify that the byte-identical guarantee is over the (intents, integer-tick) script.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Evidence is factually accurate: §6.3 L4003-4005/L4021-4023 and §6.9 L4216 describe dtTicks as "abstract active-play ticks computed by the app-layer loop from real elapsed time" without ever stating it is a whole-integer count or describing a fixed-step accumulator with sub-tick remainder. That is a genuine (minor) documentation gap. BUT the finding's headline is overstated and partly refuted: (1) the determinism guarantee is NOT silent — replay(initialState, [intents…], [tickScript…]) explicitly takes the tickScript in its signature, so replaying the recorded tick stream is the by-design contract, not a hidden dependency; (2) the combat sub-tick RNG concern is already addressed — §2.8c L1118 and §4.6.2 L2770 put the sub-tick accumulator INSIDE the deterministic CombatSim ("advances an internal sub-tick accumulator per attackSpeed," all draws from the one seeded RNG), so combat sub-stepping is internal to tick and deterministic given dtTicks; (3) the PRD nowhere claims byte-identical live play across frame rates, so no stated guarantee is violated. Net: a real but low-severity clarity gap (is dtTicks integer? where does the remainder live?), not a compromised guarantee. Resolution is the standard fixed-step accumulator pattern — a sensible-default architecture assertion, hence needs-human confirmation before writing it into the spec rather than a clerical fix.</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="refined_question">§6.3/§6.9 leave the app-layer tick cadence underspecified: dtTicks is called "abstract active-play ticks computed from real elapsed time" but it is never stated whether tick() is only ever called with whole-integer tick counts. Confirm the intended fixed-step model: the active loop accumulates real elapsed active time and dispatches only whole-integer dtTicks, carrying the fractional remainder in app-layer state (never in GameState) so GameState always rests on an integer-tick boundary and live pacing is frame-rate-independent. If yes, should one sentence be added to §6.3 (and echoed in §6.9) making this explicit, and clarifying that the byte-identical replay guarantee is over the recorded (intents, integer-tick) script? (Trade-off: writing it in pins down an architecture detail and removes ambiguity; leaving it implicit relies on the agent picking the obvious standard pattern at build time.)</refined_question>
</invoke>
confirmedFindings.question: Confirm the fixed-timestep model: the active loop accumulates real elapsed time and calls `tick` only with whole-integer dtTicks (remainder kept app-side, not persisted), making live play frame-rate-independent. Should this accumulator semantics be written into §6.3/§6.9?

confirmedFindings.title: Tier-gating spec contradicts itself: '§1.6.3 one or two required pillars' vs §4.1 three-threshold T1→T2 and T2→T3 (the v1 end-gate into the castle-town)
confirmedFindings.evidence: §1.6.3 line 408-411 states the gating model: "each tier names the one or two pillars that must clear a stated threshold to advance, and the required pillars drift per tier — early tiers require Arms + Estate ('survive and get strong'); upper tiers require Office + Name ('win it socially')". §1.6.3 T2 row (line 421) lists only "Estate + Office rising; Arms secures roads" (Arms framed as supporting, not a gate). §4.1 line 2306 repeats the pair-framing: "The required pillars drift per tier (early = Arms+Estate; upper = Office+Name)". But the §4.1 gate TABLE lists THREE hard thresholds at the two middle gates — line 2318: "| T2 → T3 (v1 end-gate) | Estate ≥ 60K · Office ≥ 50K · Arms ≥ 30K | win the region |" (and T1→T2: "Arms ≥ 5K · Estate ≥ 8K · Office ≥ 2K"). §4.1's own prose then admits the third gate, line 2330-2331: "Office enters as a required gate at T1→T2 and rises". §7.2 M5 (line 4657) re-asserts the three-pillar end-gate: "the T2→T3 end-gate (Estate ≥60K · Office ≥50K · Arms ≥30K + win-the-region, per §4.1)". The upper gates (T3→T4 = Office+Name; T4 = Name+Office) match the pair-framing; only the two middle gates carry three.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: An implementer building the gate predicate cannot tell whether Arms ≥ 30K is a HARD blocker on entering the castle-town (T3) or merely flavour ('secures roads'). It is also a design conflict with the multi-route/peaceful philosophy: if Arms is a hard gate, a pure peaceful/commercial T2 run (out-supply + out-arbitrate + settle Akagi) is still blocked from T3 until it grinds 30K Arms, which the §1.6.3 'one or two pillars, no floor' statement says should not happen.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Decide whether the middle gates intend two or three required pillars, then make §1.6.3 / §4.1-line-2306 framing and the §4.1 gate table agree. If three is intended (the table + §4.1 prose + M5 already assume it), change the design statements 'the one or two pillars' / 'early = Arms+Estate; upper = Office+Name' to 'two or three required pillars' and make §1.6.3's T2 row name Arms explicitly as a gate. If two is intended, drop the Arms ≥ 30K (and Office ≥ 2K at T1→T2) hard thresholds.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Confirmed verbatim. The framing phrase "the one or two pillars" appears at §1.6.3 (line 408) and §2.16 (line 1451), and the "early = Arms+Estate; upper = Office+Name" shorthand at §4.1 line 2306 / §1.6.3 line 410. These contradict the authoritative §4.1 gate table (lines 2317-2318), which lists THREE independent `value ≥ threshold` gates at both middle transitions: T1→T2 = Arms 5K + Estate 8K + Office 2K; T2→T3 = Estate 60K + Office 50K + Arms 30K. The three-pillar intent is reinforced, not contradicted, everywhere the actual numbers live: §4.1 prose ("Office enters as a required gate at T1→T2 and rises," line 2330), §2.16 tier tables (lines 1461-1462), and M5 (line 4657 re-asserts the 3-threshold end-gate). §1.6.3/§2.16's own T1 rows even name three pillars ("Arms + Estate, first Office") while loosely calling Arms at T2 "secures roads," which jars with the hard Arms ≥ 30K gate. Locked canon (D-005; brainstorms line 96) only says early tiers "weight Arms+Estate" — it does NOT lock "one or two," so the contradiction is internal to the PRD. Not refutable: the descriptive phrase is genuinely inconsistent with the gate tables. Severity medium rather than higher because an implementer building the predicate from §4.1's table + the `Partial<Record<pillar, threshold>>` data shape is unambiguous and unblocked; the defect is doc-coherence plus a latent design tension (peaceful-route vs a hard 30K Arms gate). Not trivial-apply: reconciling it requires a design call (reword the framing to "two or three / up to three" and explicitly name Arms as a T2 gate, OR drop the Arms 30K / Office 2K thresholds to honour the two-pillar framing), which carries a real difficulty/route-philosophy trade-off.</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="refined_question">The T1→T2 and T2→T3 gates each carry THREE hard pillar thresholds in the §4.1 table (T1→T2: Arms 5K + Estate 8K + Office 2K; T2→T3: Estate 60K + Office 50K + Arms 30K), but the canon framing in §1.6.3 / §2.16 / §4.1-line-2306 says each tier names "the one or two pillars" (early = Arms+Estate; upper = Office+Name) and describes T2's Arms as merely "securing roads." Which is intended? Option A (three is correct — already assumed by the §4.1 table+prose, §2.16 tables, and M5): reword the three "one or two pillars" framing statements to "two or three required pillars" and make the §1.6.3/§2.16 T2 rows name Arms explicitly as a gate. Trade-off: a hard Arms ≥ 30K gate forces even a pure peaceful/commercial route to grind martial deeds before entering the castle-town tier, tightening difficulty but cutting against the "no floor across pillars, win it your own way" philosophy. Option B (two is correct): drop the Arms ≥ 30K (T2→T3) and Office ≥ 2K (T1→T2) thresholds so the middle gates match the Arms+Estate framing; this loosens difficulty and re-derivation of §4.8 pacing would be needed.
confirmedFindings.question: Should the T1→T2 and T2→T3 gates require THREE pillars (the §4.1 table: Arms+Estate+Office, then Estate+Office+Arms) or TWO (per the §1.6.3 'one or two pillars / Arms+Estate / Office+Name' framing)? Trade-off: a hard Arms ≥ 30K gate at T2→T3 forces every player — including the peaceful/commercial route — to grind martial deeds before reaching the castle-town, which tightens difficulty but cuts against the 'no floor across pillars, win it your own way' design.

confirmedFindings.title: Estate stage E3 named 'Prosperous' in §5 T3.1 but the E3–E5 range glosses label E3's slot 'fortified seat' (E4's name)
confirmedFindings.evidence: §5 T3.1 line 3668 gives the explicit per-stage names: "Estate stage span: E3 Prosperous → E4 Fortified Seat." §5 T4.4 line 3800 confirms "E5 Restored-and-Surpassed." So the parked stages are E3 Prosperous, E4 Fortified Seat, E5 Restored-and-Surpassed. But every summary gloss of the E3–E5 range omits 'Prosperous' and labels the low end with E4's name: §1.5.1 line 261 "Stages E3–E5 (fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed) are parked"; §5 T2 line 3561 "(fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed) are parked for the T3/T4 build"; §7.1.3 line 4401 "Estate stages E3–E5 (fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed)".
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Update the three E3–E5 range glosses (lines 261, 3561, 4401) to read 'prosperous → fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed' (or 'prosperous → restored-and-surpassed') so they match the per-stage names in §5 T3.1 (E3 = Prosperous, not Fortified Seat).
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed verbatim. §5 T3.1 line 3668 sets the authoritative per-stage names "E3 Prosperous → E4 Fortified Seat" and §5 T4.4 line 3800 sets "E5 Restored-and-Surpassed". The E0-E2 prose (lines 258-261) follows the convention of naming every stage in the arrow chain. Yet all three E3-E5 range glosses (line 261, 3561, 4401) read "(fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed)" — they begin the span at E4's name and drop E3=Prosperous. Real, minor internal-inconsistency; clerical fix is to insert the omitted stage name in each of the three glosses (verified pair below is line 4401; identical edit applies to 261 and 3561).
confirmedFindings.old_string: | **Estate stages E3–E5** (fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed) | T3+ | §1.5.1 |
confirmedFindings.new_string: | **Estate stages E3–E5** (prosperous → fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed) | T3+ | §1.5.1 |

confirmedFindings.title: ADR D-008 still lists the rejected coinage 政威 for the Standing & Office pillar (resolved to 官威 at the §5 authenticity pass)
confirmedFindings.evidence: decisions.md D-008 line 81 lists the four pillars as "Arms (武威) · Estate & Wealth (家産) · Standing & Office (政威) · Name & Honour (家格)". But the §5 authenticity pass resolved this kanji and rejected 政威: prd.md line 386-388 "the Standing & Office pillar's kanji is RESOLVED = 官威 (kan'i)... the earlier coined 政威 was rejected", and line 3839 "the Standing & Office pillar kanji is set to 官威 (kan'i)... (coined 政威 rejected)". The locked-decisions canon (line 287-288) also fixes 官威. The four-pillar identity used by the national banzuke (§2.18) thus carries a stale kanji in the ADR that drove it.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Per the ADR 'don't delete, annotate' convention, mark the superseded coinage rather than silently swapping it: keep 政威 visible with a forward note to 官威 and the §5 authenticity pass date.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed. docs/history/decisions.md D-008 (line 81) lists the four pillars as "Arms (武威) · Estate & Wealth (家産) · Standing & Office (政威) · Name & Honour (家格)". The §5 authenticity pass (2026-06-25) RESOLVED the Standing & Office kanji to 官威 and explicitly rejected the coined 政威 — confirmed in docs/prd.md at lines 378, 386-388, 752-753, 801-802, 1436, 1702-1703, 3838-3839, and in brainstorms/2026-06-25-locked-decisions.md lines 287-288 (and lines 15/208 note 政威 was never to be committed). So the ADR that drove the four-pillar identity carries a stale, rejected coinage with no annotation, violating the repo's "don't delete, annotate" ADR convention. Real low-severity internal inconsistency. Note: the fix lives in docs/history/decisions.md (not docs/prd.md, which is already correct); verified_old/new below are byte-exact from decisions.md line 81.
confirmedFindings.old_string: **Standing & Office (政威)** · **Name & Honour (家格)**
confirmedFindings.new_string: **Standing & Office (政威 → superseded by 官威 *kan'i* at the §5 authenticity pass, 2026-06-25)** · **Name & Honour (家格)**

clusters.theme: Undefined level model blocks combat HP, the attribute-point economy, and a locked anti-cross-feed invariant
clusters.summary: Three load-bearing 'levels' are referenced but never defined or stored. `characterLevel` drives hpMax (§4.6.1) and the attribute-point cadence (§4.4: +1/2 levels) plus a 'compounding skill-XP multiplier' (§2.7), yet §4.5 defines only per-SKILL curves and §6.4's GameState stores no level/xp field — while §2.7c/§2.19 say it IS stored. A separate 'character Combat Level' (CombatDeedsPool, §2.8) is named in §1/§2 but absent from §4 and §6.4. `mobLevel` (§4.6.5 combat-XP) has no field in MobDef (§2.9c). Because the character-level XP source is undefined, freely-allocatable per-level attribute points are a potential labour→combat back door (vision-pillars-2) that the §4.5.3 type-wall doesn't cover. This blocks M2 combat and the whole build economy.

clusters.theme: Three 'persisted surface' enumerations (§6.4 GameState / §6.8 save list / §2.19) disagree and omit non-derivable state
clusters.summary: §6.4's typed GameState, §6.8's save list, and §2.19's prose persist-list contradict each other and drop fields that §6.4's own 'store only non-derivable state' rule requires: current location/area (set by the travel intent), market-saturation state (§2.4 MarketState), estate stage/built-structures/roster (§2.17), current weather/lunarPhase (§2.2), per-instance equipment durability (§2.10 — equipment is stored as a bare ItemInstanceId with no instance store), the active-dent slot (§6.4's own comment says 'dents recorded here' but the type has none), and §6.8 also omits stored tier, the live combat encounter, the event log, and current vitals. As written, market damper, estate build state, durability, dents, and location reset on reload or are unrepresentable. Several are clean clerical adds (location, dent slot, durability via EquipState, §6.8 list, Rewards bus); market-saturation/estate-stage/weather stored-vs-derived is a genuine design call.

clusters.theme: RNG sub-stream + tick model contradicts the locked named-sub-stream determinism canon
clusters.summary: Canon D-013a/H4 locks 'splitmix64 + named sub-streams (combat/loot/seasonal/world-gen) that never perturb each other' and 'persist seed + stream cursors' (plural). But §6.7 specifies a single `{ seed, counter }` whose children are 'split from the parent counter' — order-dependent, couples the streams, and cannot hold four independent cursors (only combat has its own persisted cursor). §6.7 also still offers 'mulberry32 or splitmix64' as an open choice though splitmix64 is locked (trivial). Separately, dtTicks integer-quantization / app-layer accumulator is unspecified, so the byte-identical-replay guarantee implicitly depends on a recorded tickScript. Live single-runs are still deterministic; the risk is forward-stability across content additions and reload-exact sub-stream resume.

clusters.theme: §7 milestone roadmap omits the FUN quality bar and gives no build task to several locked v1 systems
clusters.summary: §7 instruments PACING and VISUALS exhaustively but never the FUN-proxies (dead-time, reward/unlock cadence, always-a-visible-next-goal, first-5-min hook), and the §7.4.1 risk register (5 rows) never names 'the locked ~28.5h grind ships balanced but reads as a slog' — contradicting CLAUDE.md and prd_human_feedback K2/K3 which call fun the #1 gap. Three locked v1 systems also have no owning phase: the rich attribute system + character level + allocation UI (consumed by M2a combat but never built), fishing (a locked skill with contradictory T0-R5/R6 vs T1-V2 introduction), and the Name & Honour bar's first-light beat (Estate/Arms/Office each have one; Name does not).

clusters.theme: Emotional climax (Tama/Otsuru reveal + true name) gated two incompatible ways
clusters.summary: The game's stated emotional peak is documented as BOTH a guaranteed spine beat fired at G6 for every player (§3.6 G6 log line, §5 T2.2/T2.7 main-quest 'PAYOFF') AND a payoff that lands only on the fully-optional, skippable Origin track's O5 capstone (§3.6.2, gated on the Origin Ties meter; M6 verifier asserts Origin 'NEVER' gates the spine). A min-maxing spine-only player reaches G6 without O5. Determines whether the climax is guaranteed or officially missable; a builder cannot implement both.

clusters.theme: 'Conditioning' breaks the locked no-labour→combat-cross-feed invariant
clusters.summary: §1.2/§1.5.1/§2.8/§1.13 repeatedly fix conditioning as labour-built with ZERO combat stat bonus (the load-bearing mediocre-start / A3 / D-011 invariant), but §4.5.3's type-level no-cross-feed wall lists 'conditioning's combat facet' as a combat-skill milestone that may grant STR/AGI. Either reading contradicts a binding constraint; a builder encoding §4.5.3 could silently re-open the exact cross-feed the doc says it killed.

clusters.theme: T0 balance gates are internally impossible / inconsistent
clusters.summary: R6/E2 require Estate Influence ≥ 1K, but Estate accrual over all of T0 tops out at exactly 0.8K (the T0→T1 gate, reached only at R7). On an up-only curve the player hits the 0.8K tier gate before ever reaching 1K, so E2 is unbuildable and R6 unleaveable — a CI/verify pacing test driving the spine would deadlock. Mirroring Arms' R6 floor (80% of gate) suggests Estate ≈ 0.5-0.6K. Separately the §4.8.1 R5 'Arms→~0.25K' snapshot can't be produced by ~6 deeds at 8-20 ip (~120 ip max), and the 30 Arms deeds' distribution across R5-R7 is under-specified vs the 'evenly ~9-10 min per pillar' claim.

clusters.theme: Tier-gate framing: two-vs-three required pillars (is Arms a hard gate or 'secures roads'?)
clusters.summary: §1.6.3/§2.16/§4.1-prose frame gating as 'the one or two pillars' (early=Arms+Estate, upper=Office+Name) and call T2's Arms merely 'secures roads', but the §4.1 gate TABLE lists THREE hard thresholds at both middle gates (T1→T2 Arms 5K+Estate 8K+Office 2K; T2→T3 Estate 60K+Office 50K+Arms 30K), and §4.8.3's deed budget fully derives Arms 30K. An implementer can't tell if Arms ≥30K is a hard blocker into the castle-town; it also conflicts with the peaceful/commercial-route philosophy. (Held numbers-balance-4's prose fix out of trivial-apply pending this design call.)

clusters.theme: Stale superseded-spine: Kuzuhara reattaches the MC's personal tie / birthplace (violates locked 'region node only')
clusters.summary: §1.7 world table (line 463) says the drowned Kuzuhara hamlet is 'the disaster that nearly killed Tahei' and that 'the backstory and lost-child evidence resolve here', and §1.5.3 calls Kuzuhara the MC's 'birthplace' — both directly contradict the locked canon (restated 4+ times in §5 and prd_human_feedback) that Kuzuhara is a region node with NO personal tie; the MC's caravan died at the Pass and his origin resolves via the Sawatari-juku family. Timeline also rules it out (Kuzuhara drowned ~50y ago vs the MC ~18-20). Fix direction is locked, so applied as trivial.

clusters.theme: Narrative canon: find-spot, grief register, and name-collision inconsistencies
clusters.summary: Two zones are both tagged 'where he was found' (the weir vs the village boundary-stone/jizō) — destabilising the lone residual-ambiguity beat anchored to the find-spot. The origin family's grief register is 'dead' in §1.8 but 'away/lost' in §3.6.2/§5. The T0 field-lad 'Mago' is an exact prefix of locked T1 antagonist 'Magobei' (the doc de-collides similar names elsewhere). The §5 first-fight beat says he survives 'by Jūbei's arrival', contradicting the canon 'never rescued' mediocre-start beat (fixed as trivial).

clusters.theme: v1 estate scope: 'early E3' vs locked E0-E2, plus E3 stage naming and a mis-pointed park reference
clusters.summary: §3.6/§5 T2 say the estate fabric reaches 'early E3', but §2.17/§7.1.2/§4.7.5 lock v1 at E0-E2 with E3-E5 parked and no authored E3 cost. Separately, E3 is named 'Prosperous' in §5 T3.1 but the E3-E5 range glosses (§1.5.1 L261, §5 T2.4 L3561, §7.1.3 L4401) start the span at 'fortified seat' (E4's name), dropping 'Prosperous'. And §4.7.5 parks E3-E5 with a pointer to §1.5.2 (the Village web) instead of §1.5.1.

clusters.theme: Terminology overload and romanization inconsistency
clusters.summary: 'Standing' labels three distinct mechanics on the hero scoreboard: the 官威 pillar ('Standing & Office'), the Arms-pillar rank-gate ('Combat Standing'), and Arms deeds ('Arms standing'). Romanization drifts: 郷士 ships as 'goshi' 10× (should be 'gōshi' per the locked macron convention; also in frozen ADR/brainstorm canon), and 'ronin' vs '*rōnin*' is used inconsistently (~13×) — the §6.6 macron-lint is blind to both because they sit on the allow-list. No glossary/codex exists and several Edo terms (goyōkin, sankin-kōtai, han) are never glossed (all in deferred T3/T4 content).

clusters.theme: Progression-ladder reveal / gate-kind / tier-up mechanism inconsistencies
clusters.summary: §3.5's nav-reveal ledger omits the R4 Crafting tab and R5 Quest log that §3.2 reveals (nav node vs nested panel?). R5→R6 and V2→V3 are tagged RANK 'Estate Service' in the ladder but 'Estate & Wealth' PILLAR in the earned-transition spines. The tier-expansion map is placed at R6 in §3.2/§3.5 but R7 in §1.5.1/§1.12. And how `state.tier` advances is unspecified: §6.13 says 'set by the tier-up intent' but the §6.3 Intent union has no such verb and §6.3/§2.16 describe it as automatic.

clusters.theme: Combat attribute wiring gaps (INT/SPD effects, STR row, baseSpeed anchor)
clusters.summary: §4.4 promises INT '+0.5% damage vs known bestiary' and SPD 'turn-order/first-strike', but neither appears in any §4.6 formula (the turnless sub-tick sim has no first-strike; INT is a combat dump-stat in the math) — a problem under no-respec. §4.4's STR row omits STR's defense and hpMax contributions that §4.6.1 implements (trivial). §4.6.2 anchors 'baseSpeed = 1.0 at SPD 5', but the formula makes 1.0 the SPD-0 value (trivial).

clusters.theme: Stale derived/duplicate numbers diverging from authoritative tables
clusters.summary: §4.9's levers dashboard lists Name JUDGE_K as 3,000/24,000, but the §4.2.2 derivation (0.30·TIER_REF_NAME) gives 4,500/42,000 (Arms/Estate/Office all match; only Name is stale). §4.5.1's illustrative cumulative-XP figures (L10 ~2.1K, L25 ~41K, L50 ~1.5M) don't match the stated formula (should be ~1.2K / ~17K / ~1.1M). Both are clerical syncs to the authoritative source.

clusters.theme: Architecture catalog gaps: belief-beast registry and the universal Rewards object
clusters.summary: §2.9/§2.13/§2.22#5 mandate a SEPARATE belief-beast/rumour registry (the type-level guard behind 'no belief-creature in spawn tables'), but §6.5's content-registry catalog has no such module (a design call on one-type-per-module). And §6.5's 'one shape everywhere' Rewards object omits `pillarDeltas`, though §2.1/§2.12/§7 route pillar accrual — the macro spine — through that bus (trivial).

clusters.theme: Stale ADR / intent-record status markers
clusters.summary: ADR D-008 (decisions.md) still records the rejected coinage 政威 for the Standing & Office pillar, which the §5 authenticity pass resolved to 官威 (kan'i) everywhere in prd.md. And prd_human_feedback H2 is still marked deferred (⏳) although J4 marks the same §5 authenticity pass RESOLVED and it is fully applied in prd.md. Both are clerical status-marker fixes in canon/record files.

clusters.theme: Broken in-doc cross-reference links (over-climbing relative paths)
clusters.summary: The §3/§4/§7 'see canon' preambles link `../../brainstorms/2026-06-25-locked-decisions.md` (one ../ too many; 404) when the working form is `../brainstorms/...` (line 34). The §4 preamble's ADR-log link uses `../history/decisions.md` (404) vs the working `history/decisions.md` (line 4). Pure clerical path fixes.

clusters.theme: R0 pacing-test exemption omitted and local gate mislabelled 'CI'
clusters.summary: §7.1.2's pacing acceptance criterion ('any rung in < ~28 min fails') would falsely FAIL the human-blessed ~5-min R0 cold-open (exempt per §4.8.1), and labels the gate 'CI-enforced' despite the locked 'no hosted CI' constraint (it is the local verify run). Clerical alignment to already-locked decisions.

clusters.theme: Low-severity canon/fun loose ends (Origin 'ZERO gift' over-scope, Nihonbashi, T2 'new verbs')
clusters.summary: The Origin track is called 'an accelerant ... with ZERO mechanical gift' in the same clause that grants a ~10-15% speedup and a 'modest global skill-XP buff' — the ZERO-gift scope should be limited to the backstory/identity reveal (3 occurrences). 'Nihonbashi' (a real Edo district) appears in T4 prose but isn't on the §6.6 fictional-setting allow-list. T2's headline 'new verbs' (brokering/diplomacy/arbitration) are specified mostly as deed→ip reskins, risking the 'same loop, bigger numbers' slump the fun doc warns against (though a real rivals-tradeoff choice and §5d anti-slump levers exist).

human_questions.question: Define the level model and the attribute system, and close the labour→combat back door. (a) How does 'character level' accrue and on what curve (its own XP track + GameState field, or DERIVED from total skill XP)? It drives hpMax (§4.6.1), +1 attribute point per 2 levels (§4.4), and a 'compounding skill-XP multiplier' (§2.7) — none of which are computable today. (b) Is the §2.8 'character Combat Level' (CombatDeedsPool) a distinct mechanic (needs curve/effect/stored combatXp), a synonym for character level, or just framing for Arms accrual? (c) Where does mobLevel come from for the §4.6.5 combat-XP formula (an authored MobDef.level vs derived from dangerRing)? (d) May freely-allocatable per-level attribute points fund combat-feed STR/AGI if labour XP feeds the level (the §4.5.3 wall doesn't cover them)? (e) Should INT's '+0.5% damage vs bestiary' be wired into §4.6 or struck from §4.4 (it's currently a combat dump-stat under no-respec)? (f) Which milestone builds the attribute system + character-XP loop + allocation UI, since M2a consumes it but no phase builds it?
human_questions.context: Three undefined 'levels' (characterLevel, Combat Level, mobLevel) are load-bearing for HP, the build economy, combat XP, and a global multiplier; the gap blocks M2 and threatens the locked no-labour→combat-cross-feed invariant. Pick one canonical name per concept and add the missing stored fields to §6.4.
options: Character level DERIVED from total skill XP (remove from §2.19/§6.4 'stored', add derivation + multiplier formula)
Character level as its own STORED combat-sourced XP track (add field + curve; ties HP/builds to combat, cuts against peaceful-labour texture)
Merge 'Combat Level' into character level and delete the redundant term
human_questions.recommendation: Make character level its own stored track but source it from combat/deeds-inclusive XP (not raw labour) so labour-funded points can't silently raise STR/AGI; define the multiplier magnitude; add MobDef.level; assign the build to M1 (store + loop) with the allocation/combat read at the head of M2a.

human_questions.question: Pin the RNG sub-stream model and persisted shape. Canon D-013a/H4 lock 'splitmix64 + named sub-streams (combat/loot/seasonal/world-gen) that never perturb each other' and 'persist seed + stream cursors' (plural), but §6.7 specifies a single `{ seed, counter }` whose children are 'split from the parent counter' (order-dependent; couples the streams; one counter can't hold four cursors). Which scheme? Also confirm the fixed-timestep tick model: does the app loop call tick() only with whole-integer dtTicks (remainder kept app-side) so live play is frame-rate-independent and the byte-identical guarantee is over the recorded (intents, integer-tick) script?
human_questions.context: The whole 'byte-identical replay / saved fight resumes identically' promise and forward-stability across content additions rest on this. Then update §6.7/§6.4/§6.8/§2.19 to match.
options: Each named stream carries its own persisted cursor in GameState (RngState = { seed, cursors: Record<StreamName, number> }); matches canon literally
Streams re-derived deterministically per (name, epoch) and consumed within one tick, so only the master cursor + combat's CombatEncounterState cursor persist (simpler save)
human_questions.recommendation: Adopt per-named-stream cursors derived as splitmix64(hash(seed, name)) with each cursor monotonic and persisted; keep mid-fight combat RNG in CombatEncounterState. Add the integer-dtTicks accumulator sentence to §6.3/§6.9.

human_questions.question: Reconcile the persisted surface and content catalog. For each of {market-saturation state, estate stage/built-structures/roster, current weather + lunarPhase}, is it a STORED non-derivable field to add to §6.4/§6.8, or DERIVED/flag-encoded and struck from §2.19? (Market saturation and durability look clearly stored — durability is being applied as a trivial fix; weather and estate-stage are the genuine calls.) Separately, where does the canon-mandated SEPARATE belief-beast/rumour registry live in §6.5's catalog (one combined module vs one-type-per-module rows)?
human_questions.context: §6.4's 'store only non-derivable state' rule is contradicted by §2.19/§6.8: market saturation evolves per-day and has nowhere to live; estate/weather may be derivable. §6.5 omits the belief-beast registry that the 'no belief-creature in spawn tables' type-guard depends on.
options: Add `market: MarketState` + `estate: EstateStage`(+roster) to §6.4/§6.8 (store)
Encode estate state in flags/producers and re-derive weather from a day-keyed RNG sub-stream (derive; remove from §2.19)
human_questions.recommendation: Store market saturation (clearly non-derivable) and durability; derive weather from the day-keyed RNG sub-stream; decide estate-stage explicitly (flag-encoded is cleanest) — then make all three lists agree. Add a `content/beliefBeasts.ts` row (or a lore module) tagged as the source the §6.6 verifier reads.

human_questions.question: Wire FUN into §7 and assign the unowned locked systems. Should §7 add (a) a risk row 'R6 — the locked ~28.5h grind ships balanced but reads as a slog / has dead-time / no visible next goal' and (b) explicit fun-proxy instrumentation tasks (dead-time, reward/unlock cadence, always-a-visible-next-goal, first-5-min hook) in M1/M3/M6 alongside the pacing harness, cross-referencing fun-factor.md and qa-playtesting.md? And: which tier/milestone owns fishing (the §2.6 T0-R5/R6 vs roadmap T1-V2 contradiction), and does M4 own the Name & Honour bar's first-light (it first scores at the first T1 season per §4.2.2)?
human_questions.context: Fun is the project's #1 stated quality bar (CLAUDE.md, prd_human_feedback K2/K3) but is owned only by living docs and never gated by the milestone plan. Fishing (a locked skill) and the Name bar's reveal have no owning phase.
options: Amend §7 to own fun-proxies + a fun risk row, and assign fishing/Name explicitly
Leave fun to living docs only and accept it isn't milestone-gated
human_questions.recommendation: Add the fun risk row and fun-proxy tasks; resolve fishing to T1-V2 (where the ford actually opens) and correct §2.6; give M4 the Name bar first-light test.

human_questions.question: Is the Tama/Otsuru truth reveal + Tahei claiming his true name SPINE-GUARANTEED at G6 for every player, or genuinely GATED behind completing the optional Origin track (O5) and therefore officially missable?
human_questions.context: This is the stated emotional peak of T2. §3.6 G6 narrates it for everyone, but §3.6.2/M6 gate it on the optional Origin-Ties meter; a min-maxer hits G6 without O5. A builder cannot implement both, and it determines whether the warmest payoff is guaranteed or skippable.
options: Spine-guaranteed at G6; Origin track only adds depth/warmth (reunions, Osen, Jinpachi)
Origin-gated at O5; officially missable
human_questions.recommendation: Make it spine-guaranteed at G6 (protect the game's emotional heart for all players); recast O5 as the Origin REUNION completing (warmth/buff) that echoes the beat. Then align §1.5.3, §3.6, §3.6.2, §5 T2.2/T2.7, and the M6 verifier, and ensure it fires exactly once.

human_questions.question: What is 'conditioning', exactly — a single labour-built enablement gate granting ZERO combat stats, or a combat drill skill that grants STR/AGI, or two distinct things? §1/§2.8 call it labour-built with ZERO combat bonus while §4.5.3's type-wall lists 'conditioning's combat facet' as granting STR/AGI.
human_questions.context: This is the load-bearing mediocre-start / no-hidden-edge invariant (A3, D-011). The contradiction would let a builder type conditioning as 'combat' and re-open the exact labour→combat cross-feed the PRD says it killed.
options: Single labour-built ZERO-bonus gate — drop 'conditioning's combat facet' from §4.5.3's STR/AGI list and type it non-combat
A combat drill skill that grants STR/AGI — stop calling it 'labour-built' in §1/§2.8
Two separately-named things: a labour capacity gate + a combat drill skill
human_questions.recommendation: Option (a) or (c): keep conditioning as a labour-built ZERO-bonus gate and, if a combat-drill stat path is wanted, give it a distinct name so the type-level wall is unambiguous.

human_questions.question: Fix the T0 balance gates and the tier-pillar-count framing. (a) What should the E2/R6 Estate floor be? It's set at 1K but Estate accrual over all of T0 maxes at 0.8K (the next-rung T0→T1 gate), making E2 unbuildable and R6 unleaveable — Arms' R6 floor is 80% of its gate, which for Estate gives ~0.64K. (b) Do the T1→T2 and T2→T3 gates require THREE pillars (the §4.1 table: +Office, +Arms 30K — already assumed by the deed budgets and M5) or TWO (per the §1.6.3 'one or two pillars / Arms secures roads' framing)? (c) How do the 30 T0 Arms deeds (350 ip) distribute across R5/R6/R7, and is R5's 'Arms→~0.25K' snapshot a typo (~6 deeds at 8-20 ip give only ~120 ip)?
human_questions.context: (a) is logically impossible as written and would deadlock a CI pacing run. (b) a hard Arms ≥30K gate forces even a pure peaceful/commercial route to grind martial deeds, cutting against the 'win it your own way' philosophy; but removing it voids the §4.8.3 21K-Arms-deed budget.
options: Lower E2/R6 Estate floor to ~0.5-0.6K (apply at §4.7.5 and §4.8.1)
Keep 3 required pillars and reword §1.6.3/§2.16 framing to 'two or three' (naming Arms as a T2 gate)
Drop the Arms 30K / Office 2K thresholds to honour the 2-pillar framing (re-derive §4.8 pacing)
human_questions.recommendation: Set E2/R6 Estate floor ≈ 0.6K. Keep three pillars (matches the authored deed budgets) and tighten the §1.6.3/§4.8.3 prose to stop implying only two. Fix R5's Arms snapshot to ~0.1K and enumerate the R5/R6/R7 Arms-deed split.

human_questions.question: Does v1's T2 reach the start of E3 (requires authoring an E3 estate stage + a §4.7.5 cost and removing E3 from the parked list), or does v1 cap the estate at E2 with E3-E5 fully parked (then drop 'early E3' from §3.6/§5 T2)? And confirm the canonical E3-E5 names (likely E3 Prosperous, E4 Fortified Seat, E5 Restored-and-Surpassed) so the three range glosses can be aligned.
human_questions.context: §3.6/§5 T2 promise 'early E3' but §2.17/§7.1.2/§4.7.5 lock E0-E2 with no authored E3 cost — a builder following the locked scope ships E2. The E3-E5 glosses also mislabel E3's slot with E4's name.
options: Cap at E2; drop 'early E3' phrasing
Author an E3 stage + cost; un-park E3
human_questions.recommendation: Cap v1 at E2 Recovering (matches the only authored cost table and locked scope); change 'early E3' to 'E2 Recovering' in §3.6/§5 T2; keep E3-E5 as parked T3+ flavour with corrected names.

human_questions.question: Resolve terminology and romanization. (a) The word 'Standing' labels three mechanics (the 官威 'Standing & Office' pillar, the Arms rank-gate 'Combat Standing', and 'Arms standing' deeds) — which keeps 'Standing' and what do the others become? (b) Romanization policy: normalize 郷士 to 'gōshi' project-wide (incl. frozen ADR/brainstorm canon) per the macron lock, or bless 'goshi' as an exception? Pick one canonical form for 'ronin'/'rōnin' too. (c) Is an in-game glossary/codex or a §6.6 'gloss-on-first-use' verifier needed for Edo terms (goyōkin, sankin-kōtai, han — all T3/T4 content)?
human_questions.context: The §6.6 macron-lint is blind to allow-listed terms, so gōshi/ronin drift will silently ship. The 'Standing' overload sits on the four-pillar hero scoreboard.
options: Rename the Arms rank-gate 'Combat Standing' → 'Combat Rank'/'Martial Prowess' so 'Standing & Office' owns the word
Rename the 官威 pillar's English label instead
Macronize gōshi/rōnin everywhere
Add a gloss-on-first-use rule or in-game codex
human_questions.recommendation: Rename the Arms rank-gate to 'Combat Rank' (cheapest disambiguation); macronize gōshi project-wide and pick one ronin form; defer glossary to when T3/T4 is built (v1 terms are already glossed).

human_questions.question: Settle the progression-ladder reveal/gate spec. (a) Are the R4 Crafting tab and R5 Quest log new top-level nav nodes (add to the §3.5 ladder) or panels nested under an existing screen (soften §3.2's 'tab'/'log' wording)? (b) Are R5→R6 and V2→V3 gated by the Estate-Service RANK meter (reword the §3.2.1/§3.4.1 spines) or the Estate & Wealth PILLAR (promote them in §3.9)? (c) Is the tier-expansion map one object at R6 (fix §1.5.1/§1.12) or a distinct R7 strategic map? (d) How is `state.tier` committed — an explicit `advance_tier` intent or an auto-commit in reduce when thresholds+story-gate are met?
human_questions.context: These are spec-clarity inconsistencies between the detailed §3 ladder and the §1 summaries / §6 contract; (d) changes the reducer contract and DEV-API surface.
human_questions.recommendation: Treat Crafting/Quests as nested panels (soften §3.2 wording); reword R6/V3 spines to 'Estate-Service threshold' (match the RANK tags + §3.9); place the map at R6 in all four spots; and make tier-up an auto-commit in reduce when the story-gate flag + thresholds are met, removing the 'tier-up intent' wording.

human_questions.question: Settle the narrative canon loose ends. (a) Was the protagonist found at the weir or the village boundary-stone/jizō (both tagged 'where he was found'; the lone residual-ambiguity offering is anchored to the boundary-stone)? (b) Did the origin family believe Tahei DEAD (§1.8) or AWAY/LOST (§3.6.2/§5)? (c) Rename the T0 field-lad 'Mago' to remove the prefix collision with locked antagonist 'Magobei'? (d) Confirm the Kuzuhara no-personal-tie rewrite (applied as a trivial fix).
human_questions.context: (a) destabilises the single A2 ambiguity beat; (b) is the difference between 'back from the dead' and 'long-absent returned' for the game's warmest payoff; (c) risks conflating a friendly peer with a named antagonist.
options: Co-locate the jizō at the weir (one find-spot)
Pick one grief register and apply across §1.8/§3.6.2/§5
Rename the field-lad (e.g. Tasuke/Kuma/Hachi)
human_questions.recommendation: Co-locate the boundary-jizō at the weir/ford; pick 'presumed dead → back from the dead' (higher catharsis) and apply consistently; rename the field-lad.

human_questions.question: Low-severity canon/fun calls: (a) Rescope the Origin track's 'ZERO mechanical gift' phrasing (3 occurrences) to mean only 'no retroactive stat/recipe/tool/combat bonus from the backstory reveal' — since the track DOES grant the ~10-15% speedup and a 'modest global skill-XP buff'; confirm the skill-XP-buff framing is intended. (b) Add 'Nihonbashi' to the §6.6 allow-list (a district of already-allowed Edo) or genericize it to 'the capital's banzuke'? (c) Do T2's brokering/diplomacy/arbitration verbs need a distinct 'contested meter' mechanic spec (§2.15), or do the existing rivals-tradeoff choice + §5d anti-slump levers suffice?
human_questions.context: These are contained flavour/scope nits, but (a) feeds the §6.6 machine-checked invariants and (c) touches the longest tier's freshness.
human_questions.recommendation: Rescope the ZERO-gift phrase to the identity reveal; add Nihonbashi to the allow-list (or genericize); accept T2's social verbs as deed-framed but rely on the rivals-tradeoff + seasonal/cross-pillar levers (no new mechanic required).

cross_cutting_risks: The level/XP model is the single biggest systemic hole: 'character level', 'Combat Level', and 'mobLevel' are all referenced but undefined and (for the first two) unstored, yet they feed HP, the attribute-point economy, combat XP, and a global skill-XP multiplier — and the undefined XP source is a potential labour→combat back door that the §4.5.3 type-wall and the locked mediocre-start invariant don't cover. Blocks M2.

Three 'persisted surface' enumerations (§2.19 prose, §6.4 typed GameState, §6.8 save list) are mutually inconsistent and omit non-derivable state (location, market saturation, estate stage/roster, weather, durability, dents, tier, live combat, log, vitals) — a structural save/resume risk that recurs across ~10 findings; §6.4's own 'store only non-derivable state' rule is violated.

The locked determinism guarantee (splitmix64 named sub-streams that never perturb each other) is contradicted by the actual §6.7 single-counter / split-from-parent spec, a stale mulberry32 option, and an unspecified tick-quantization model — putting byte-identical replay and forward-stable saves at risk.

Pervasive 'summary/dashboard vs authoritative table' drift (Name JUDGE_K, XP-curve figures, E3 stage names, kanji 政威, Arms-gate framing, two-vs-three pillar counts) — the doc duplicates derived data instead of generating it, violating the project's 'single source of truth — generate, don't duplicate' rule, so multiple places silently diverge from source.

Fun — the project's explicitly #1 quality bar — is owned only by living docs and is never wired into the §7 milestone plan or risk register; the locked ~28.5h grind could pass every verify-green pacing/visual gate while shipping 'balanced but a slog', with several locked systems (attributes, fishing, Name bar) also unassigned to any build phase.

Stale superseded-spine remnants leak cut canon back in (Kuzuhara personal tie/birthplace, the Jūbei 'rescue', the rejected 政威 glyph) — a builder reading any one summary in isolation could reintroduce content the human explicitly rejected; the locked decisions are correct but the propagation is incomplete.
synthesis.coverage_notes: "Audit operated on docs/prd.md text plus cross-doc canon (decisions.md, prd_human_feedback.md, brainstorms locked-decisions). No code exists yet (pre-M0), so all save/RNG/level/architecture findings are spec-level and could not be validated against an implementation. CORRECTIONS MADE DURING SYNTHESIS: (1) Three findings shipped malformed old/new fields (old=fragment, new=full block) that would corrupt the file if applied verbatim — combat-math-7 (cell vs full row), systems-catalog-6 (inner-braces vs whole clause), and cross-references-2 (path vs surrounding prose); all three were corrected to clean replacements and the load-bearing ones verified against the file (lines 2653, 4107, 2223). (2) save-determinism-2's single-line old_string did not match the file's line-broken §6.8 list (lines 4170-4172); reconstructed as a verified block-level edit. (3) narrative-canon-1 shipped EMPTY structured old/new fields (strings were trapped in verdict prose); reconstructed and verified verbatim against line 463 — included despite being a substantive prose rewrite because it only propagates LOCKED canon. (4) tech-architecture-1 and save-determinism-3 are the same missing-current-location issue with two different field names/positions; applied once (tech-architecture-1, currentArea after the clock line) to avoid adding the field twice. DEFERRED/UNVERIFIED IN THIS PASS: numbers-balance-4 is class trivial-apply but was withheld from trivial_fixes and routed to Q7, because endgame-coherence-1 frames the same Arms-gate as an open 2-vs-3-pillar design fork — applying its directional prose fix would prejudge the human decision. The E3-naming gloss fix (endgame-coherence-2) only covers the §7.1.3 occurrence (line 4401); the identical 'fortified seat → restored-and-surpassed' gloss also appears at §1.5.1 (~L261) and §5 T2.4 (~L3561) and needs the same edit, but exact strings for those two were not captured. cross-references-1's path fix occurs 6× (lines 1715/2225/4319) and requires replace-all, but the trivial_fixes schema has no replace_all flag. The kanji ADR fix (endgame-coherence-3) and the H2 marker fix (intent-fidelity-2) target docs/history/decisions.md and docs/prd_human_feedback.md respectively; their exact line text was not re-verified against those files in this pass. Several other passed-through trivial strings (numbers-balance-2/3, combat-math-6, tech-architecture-4/5, save-determinism-5, scope-pacing-r0-1, narrative-canon-2, vision-pillars-3, cross-references-3) were trusted from high-confidence lenses but only a subset were re-read against the file. NOT INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED: the arithmetic in numbers-balance-1/3 and intent-fidelity-1, and all claims about the fun-factor.md / qa-playtesting.md living docs (only their existence and the §7 omission were checked)."
